proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 310
; regressions is a .length count (non-negative integer)
; Guard: if (regressions > 0) — integer > 0 implies >= 1
; Prove: regressions >= 1 at this line

(declare-const regressions Int)
(declare-const line_310_executes Int)

; Transition: regressions = changes.filter(c => c.type === "regressed").length >= 0
(assert (>= regressions 0))
; Transition: guarded by if (regressions > 0)
(assert (=> (= line_310_executes 1) (> regressions 0)))

; Attempt to falsify: regression message fires with regressions < 1
(assert (= line_310_executes 1))
(assert (< regressions 1))
(check-sat)

; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 310
; ternary: regressions === 1 ? "" : "s"
; Prove: suffix is "" iff regressions == 1; suffix is "s" iff regressions > 1
; (regressions < 1 is ruled out by the path-condition proof above)

(declare-const regressions Int)
(declare-const suffix_is_plural Int)  ; 1 = "s" appended, 0 = "" appended

; Transition: path condition guarantees regressions >= 1
(assert (>= regressions 1))
; Transition: ternary selects suffix
(assert (=> (= regressions 1) (= suffix_is_plural 0)))
(assert (=> (> regressions 1) (= suffix_is_plural 1)))

; Attempt to falsify: singular suffix used while regressions > 1
(assert (= suffix_is_plural 0))
(assert (> regressions 1))
(check-sat)
